notes: VACUUM INTO is SQLite-specific; Turso/libSQL does not support it. Checkpoint creation must use alternative serialization (e.g., file copy or backup API).
VACUUM INTO on attached database (active_vec). Turso lacks ATTACH and VACUUM INTO; vector shard snapshot needs complete reimplementation.
Used to resolve main/active_vec paths. Turso has no ATTACH or PRAGMA database_list; shard path resolution must be externalized.
Queries temp.sqlite_master for temp table detection. Turso may not expose temp schema the same way.
Queries attached database sqlite_master. Turso lacks ATTACH; attached-database introspection must be replaced.
FTS5 'delete-all' command. Turso/libSQL lacks FTS5; this must be reimplemented with external search or alternative FTS.
FTS5 'rebuild' command used in post-restore derived artifact rebuilds. FTS5 not available in Turso.
CREATE TRIGGER for append-only enforcement. Turso supports triggers but syntax may differ; verify compatibility.
SAVEPOINT used for atomic merge-restore of individual tables. Turso supports savepoints but verify semantics.
WAL checkpoint pragma. Turso/libSQL may use different WAL or no WAL; post-delete maintenance needs adaptation.

symbol: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5
notes: FTS5 virtual table with content-sync. Turso has no FTS5 and no loadExtension; schema downgrade path must remove FTS entirely.
FTS5 content-sync triggers. All three triggers reference FTS5 commands; entire FTS sync layer must be replaced for Turso.
PRAGMA .pragma() method is better-sqlite3 specific. Turso uses SET statements; foreign_keys toggle needs API change.
Dynamic ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN for schema evolution. Turso supports ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN but verify column-default semantics.
Manual BEGIN IMMEDIATE for write-lock acquisition. Turso's distributed locking model differs; explicit transaction begin may behave differently.
INDEXED BY hint for partial index usage. Turso may not support INDEXED BY hints or partial indexes identically.
PRAGMA incremental_vacuum after auto_vacuum=2. Turso/libSQL has different space reclamation; .pragma() API unavailable.

Three pragma calls for vacuum and WAL checkpoint post-delete maintenance; Turso has no .pragma() and no WAL — must remove or replace with Turso compaction API
FTS5 optimize command after bulk deletes; Turso has no FTS5 — must remove or replace
Complex merge transaction: retire predecessor, insert merged row, insert vector, insert causal edge, BM25 update — all in one sync transaction
Compare-and-swap pattern inside sync transaction; Turso remote transactions may have different latency/locking semantics
